note: dashboard.html only. No changes to dashboard_server.py or any other file.

bugs: Bankroll KPI shows cost-basis (start + realized PnL), not mark-to-market (available_cash + live_equity_total)
Capital Committed tile in LIVE cash strip always shows $— because the div has no id
Feed status shows 'Market feed stale · 0s ago' which is contradictory — stream is live (0s ago) but marks are old

fix1_committed_tile_missing_id.where: Line 425 — the Capital Committed cash-box in the LIVE tab cash-strip
fix1_committed_tile_missing_id.current:
Capital Committed (entry cost)
$—
fix1_committed_tile_missing_id.change_to:
Capital Committed (entry cost)
$—
fix1_committed_tile_missing_id.note: The command bar already has id='cash-committed' bound correctly. Use a different id ('live-committed') for the LIVE tab tile to avoid collision.

fix2_bind_live_committed_in_updateState.where: In updateState(s) function, near line 668 where cash-committed is already bound
fix2_bind_live_committed_in_updateState.note: live-committed and cash-committed get the same value. live-committed is the LIVE tab tile; cash-committed is the command bar.

fix3_module_level_available_cash_variabl.where: Near the top of the <script> block where other module-level state variables are declared (look for 'let stateStream', 'let latestStreamTs', etc.)
fix3_module_level_available_cash_variabl.note: Bridge between updateState() and applyStreamPositionsMark(). Needed for MTM bankroll calculation.

fix4_store_available_cash_in_updateState.where: In updateState(s), immediately after the bankroll block (near line 667)
fix4_store_available_cash_in_updateState.note: Stores available_cash so the SSE handler can compute MTM without re-fetching state.

fix5_bankroll_mtm_in_sse_handler.where: In applyStreamPositionsMark(), inside the 'if (payload?.live_equity_total != null)' block (lines 1039-1042)
fix5_bankroll_mtm_in_sse_handler.note: MTM bankroll = available_cash (from last state poll) + live_equity_total (from SSE). This replaces the cost-basis value set by updateState(). When SSE is not yet connected, kpi-bankroll still shows br.current as a fallback from updateState().

fix6_feed_status_wording.where: In applyStreamPositionsMark(), lines 1045-1051 — the bot-status-text update block
fix6_feed_status_wording.note: 'prices stale' means mark data is old (>15s). Dropping '· Xs ago' because latestStreamTs measures SSE message recency (always ~0s when connected), not price age — the two clocks created a contradiction. 'Market feed live' confirms the stream is connected; 'prices stale' flags the data quality separately.

steps: Open dashboard in browser, check LIVE tab cash strip — Committed tile shows $150.00 (not $—)
Command bar Committed also shows $150.00 — unchanged, still works
Command bar Bankroll shows available_cash + live_equity (e.g. ~$734 not ~$839)
When SSE stale=true: banner shows 'Market feed live · prices stale' (not 'stale · 0s ago')
When SSE stale=false: banner shows 'Market feed live'
Bankroll updates when position marks change (SSE drives it, not just state poll)

do_not: Edit any file other than dashboard.html
Restart the process (process restart is not required for dashboard.html changes — browser hard-refresh is sufficient)
